core_mechanism: Bounding the multiplicity of a Laplacian operator's low-lying eigenvalue by a topological invariant of the underlying space, realized by using that eigenvalue's eigenspace to embed the space 'tightly' into a Euclidean space of matching dimension, with the eigenfunctions' nodal (sign-change) domains constraining the embedding (a Courant-type nodal / Colin-de-Verdiere spectral-topology mechanism).
brief_reason: The abstract links the multiplicity m of the second Laplacian eigenvalue to a tight mapping of the graph into m-dimensional Euclidean space and to a discrete analogue of Courant's nodal line theorem, with m capped by a topological (genus/chromatic-number) upper bound.
